generatedSentence: Within the Ninth Orbital Hegemony, a janitor named Jax worked rigidly, observing the innovation through a kiosk. His quiet voice, often subject to disparage, collected backups of the system's efficiency, stored in a hidden gallery. He knew the fluids powering their transporters, the fonts displaying their power, all magnified the corporate control. Jax, feeling plucked from a forgotten past, anticipated payback, remembering a broken handshake, a marshmallow dream washed away by the rains, as officials jockeyed for power in their vivarium. Jax, now a rebel, felt a new, different force urging him. He refused to kneel before the abject system. His association with the resistance was now public, a kick-ass move that caused a system overload. He knew the familiar sight of corporate loaders, decked out like a unicorn, was a facade. They lightly trampled dissent, trading freedoms for control. The humid air hung heavy as heckling filled the kilometers to the factory. He wore a simple wrist loop, a signal of dynamite ready to blowout, for their brutal game of cricket. His name, now a signal for rebellion, engaged fellow operatives. Policemen, some undercover, objected fiercely, but Jax's team had new utilities. Imagine, they kayaked through the city's flooded arteries, towing disabled corporate vehicles, each millimeter of progress a satisfaction. A display of their strength, a tango with danger, they docked near the factory. Jax lit the wick of a hidden device, its validations glowing. Their harvest of data would kick-start the revolution, beyond what any ordained system could foresee. The conclusion saw the anisotropic valve of the system finally ruptured. Jax, knighted by the people, beheld the clear windows of the now-liberated factory, no longer traumatized by its past. The gawky invention of freedom, a giant balloon resembling a jasmine-scented unicorn, floated over the racetrack. He'd shown them the lowdown, forced them to reject their own hegemony. His old revolver, a memory of a prank now ended, was washed clean. The imported ivory of the former rulers was gone, leaving only the julep of victory and the memory of a single nanosecond when everything changed.
panelSentences: Within the Ninth Orbital Hegemony, a janitor named Jax worked rigidly, observing the innovation through a kiosk. His quiet voice, often subject to disparage, collected backups of the system's efficiency, stored in a hidden gallery. He knew the fluids powering their transporters, the fonts displaying their power, all magnified the corporate control. Jax, feeling plucked from a forgotten past, anticipated payback, remembering a broken handshake, a marshmallow dream washed away by the rains, as officials jockeyed for power in their vivarium.

Jax, now a rebel, felt a new, different force urging him. He refused to kneel before the abject system. His association with the resistance was now public, a kick-ass move that caused a system overload. He knew the familiar sight of corporate loaders, decked out like a unicorn, was a facade. They lightly trampled dissent, trading freedoms for control. The humid air hung heavy as heckling filled the kilometers to the factory. He wore a simple wrist loop, a signal of dynamite ready to blowout, for their brutal game of cricket.

His name, now a signal for rebellion, engaged fellow operatives. Policemen, some undercover, objected fiercely, but Jax's team had new utilities. Imagine, they kayaked through the city's flooded arteries, towing disabled corporate vehicles, each millimeter of progress a satisfaction. A display of their strength, a tango with danger, they docked near the factory. Jax lit the wick of a hidden device, its validations glowing. Their harvest of data would kick-start the revolution, beyond what any ordained system could foresee.

The conclusion saw the anisotropic valve of the system finally ruptured. Jax, knighted by the people, beheld the clear windows of the now-liberated factory, no longer traumatized by its past. The gawky invention of freedom, a giant balloon resembling a jasmine-scented unicorn, floated over the racetrack. He'd shown them the lowdown, forced them to reject their own hegemony. His old revolver, a memory of a prank now ended, was washed clean. The imported ivory of the former rulers was gone, leaving only the julep of victory and the memory of a single nanosecond when everything changed.
rawPanelSentences: Within the Ninth Orbital Hegemony, a janitor named Jax worked rigidly, observing the innovation through a kiosk. His quiet voice, often subject to disparage, collected backups of the system's efficiency, stored in a hidden gallery. He knew the fluids powering their transporters, the fonts displaying their power, all magnified the corporate control. Jax, feeling plucked from a forgotten past, anticipated payback, remembering a broken handshake, a marshmallow dream washed away by the rains, as officials jockeyed for power in their vivarium.

Jax, now a rebel, felt a new, different force urging him. He refused to kneel before the abject system. His association with the resistance was now public, a kick-ass move that caused a system overload. He knew the familiar sight of corporate loaders, decked out like a unicorn, was a facade. They lightly trampled dissent, trading freedoms for control. The humid air hung heavy as heckling filled the kilometers to the factory. He wore a simple wrist loop, a signal of dynamite ready to blowout, for their brutal game of cricket.

His name, now a signal for rebellion, engaged fellow operatives. Policemen, some undercover, objected fiercely, but Jax's team had new utilities. Imagine, they kayaked through the city's flooded arteries, towing disabled corporate vehicles, each millimeter of progress a satisfaction. A display of their strength, a tango with danger, they docked near the factory. Jax lit the wick of a hidden device, its validations glowing. Their harvest of data would kick-start the revolution, beyond what any ordained system could foresee.

The conclusion saw the anisotropic valve of the system finally ruptured. Jax, knighted by the people, beheld the clear windows of the now-liberated factory, no longer traumatized by its past. The gawky invention of freedom, a giant balloon resembling a jasmine-scented unicorn, floated over the racetrack. He'd shown them the lowdown, forced them to reject their own hegemony. His old revolver, a memory of a prank now ended, was washed clean. The imported ivory of the former rulers was gone, leaving only the julep of victory and the memory of a single nanosecond when everything changed.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a grizzled, cybernetically enhanced janitor, wearing a worn-out jumpsuit with glowing circuits and a determined expression. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, a lone figure, observing a bustling futuristic cityscape from a shadowy kiosk window, slight low-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, now in tactical gear, leading a small, determined group through a rain-soaked, neon-lit alleyway, dynamic mid-shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, piloting a makeshift kayak through submerged futuristic city streets, towing a disabled corporate vehicle, action-oriented wide shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, standing triumphantly atop a liberated, newly illuminated factory building, looking out over a city celebrating with floating balloons, heroic high-angle shot.

definitions.definition: A member of a Christian religious community of women who live by certain vows and usually wear a habit, (specifically) those living together in a cloister.
(by extension) A member of a similar female community in other confessions.
A prostitute.
A kind of pigeon with the feathers on its head like the hood of a nun.
The fourteenth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
Inequality in marriage; marriage with an inferior.
To match unequally; to degrade or dishonor.
To dishonor by a comparison with what is inferior; to lower in rank or estimation by actions or words; to speak slightingly of; to depreciate; to undervalue.
To ridicule, mock, discredit.
A species of mallow, Althaea officinalis, that grows in marshy terrain.

A type of confectionery, originally (since Ancient Egyptian times) made from this plant, but now generally made of sugar or corn syrup, gelatin that has been pre-softened in water, gum arabic, flavorings, and sometimes beaten egg whites, all whipped to a spongy consistency.
Someone who is soft and benign.
definitions.example: He acts like a total badass, but he's really just a marshmallow once you get to know him.

definitions.definition: To soften (literally or figuratively)
A place artificially arranged for keeping or raising living animals.
Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character
definitions.example: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.

definitions.definition: Sound made through vibration of the vocal cords; sonant, or intonated, utterance; tone; — distinguished from mere breath sound as heard in whispering and voiceless consonants.
The tone or sound emitted by an object
The faculty or power of utterance
definitions.example: to cultivate the voice

definitions.definition: That which is communicated; message; meaning.
An expressed opinion, choice, will, desire, or wish; the right or ability to make such expression or to have it considered
Command; precept.
One who speaks; a speaker.
A particular style or way of writing that expresses a certain tone or feeling.

(grammar) A particular way of inflecting or conjugating verbs, or a particular form of a verb, by means of which is indicated the relation of the subject of the verb to the action which the verb expresses.
definitions.example: The verbal system of Latin has two voices, active and passive.

definitions.definition: In harmony, an independent vocal or instrumental part in a piece of composition.
definitions.example: The theme of this piece constantly migrates between the three voice parts.

definitions.definition: (IRC) A flag associated with a user on a channel, determining whether or not they can send messages to the channel.
An act of revenge.
definitions.example: They beat us last year, so this year's win was payback.

definitions.definition: A benefit, reward, a form of recompense.
A return on investment
A refund, reimbursement
The grasping of hands by two people when greeting, leave-taking, or making an agreement.
An exchange of signals between two devices when communications begin in order to ensure synchronization.
To perform a handshake; to shake hands.
To perform a handshake with another device.
Domination, influence, or authority over another, especially by one political group over a society or by one nation over others.
Dominance of one social group over another, such that the ruling group or hegemon acquires some degree of consent from the subordinate, as opposed to dominance purely by force.
The extent to which time is well used for the intended task.
definitions.example: The efficiency of the planning department is deplorable.

definitions.definition: The quality of producing an effect or effects.
The extent to which a resource, such as electricity, is used for the intended purpose; the ratio of useful work to energy expended.
definitions.example: The efficiency of this loudspeaker is 40%.

definitions.definition: A one-room apartment.
definitions.example: I have an efficiency available June through July.

definitions.definition: An institution, building, or room for the exhibition and conservation of works of art.
An establishment that buys, sells, and displays works of art.
The uppermost seating area projecting from the rear or side walls of a theater, concert hall, or auditorium.
The, often elevated and in the rear, part of a courtroom where seating for the public audience is facilitated during trial.
A roofed promenade, especially one extending along the wall of a building and supported by arches or columns on the outer side
The spectators of an event, collectively.
A browsable collection of images, font styles, etc.

definitions.definition: (fortification) A covered passage cut through the earth or masonry.
A level or drive in a mine.
To show off
In a rigid manner; stiffly.
Makeup for the lips.

definitions.definition: A stick of this make-up.

definitions.definition: To apply lipstick to; to paint with lipstick.
The person or thing in the ninth position.
One of nine equal parts of a whole.

The compound interval between any tone and the tone represented on the ninth degree of the staff above it, as between one of the scale and two of the octave above; the octave of the second, consisting of 13 or 14 semitones (called minor and major ninth).
To lose a ninth
To divide by nine
The ordinal form of the number nine.
To praise, glorify (someone or something, especially God).
To make (something) larger or more important.
To make (someone or something) appear greater or more important than it is; to intensify, exaggerate.
To make (something) appear larger by means of a lens, magnifying glass, telescope etc.
To have effect; to be of importance or significance.

definitions.definition: A copy of a file or record, stored separately from the original, that can be used to recover the original if it is destroyed or damaged.

definitions.definition: An accumulation of material caused by a (partial) obstruction or (complete) blockage of the flow or movement of the material, or an accumulation of material that causes an overflow due to the flow being greater than the maximum possible flow.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) reinforcements

definitions.definition: To pull something sharply; to pull something out

definitions.definition: To take or remove (someone) quickly from a particular place or situation.

definitions.definition: To remove feathers from a bird.

definitions.definition: To cause dephosphorization of (molten pig iron) by adding substances containing iron oxide, and sometimes manganese oxide.
To pass (a gas or gaseous mixture) through or over a liquid for the purpose of purifying it, especially by removing soluble constituents.
A handgun with a revolving chamber enabling several shots to be fired without reloading.
(by extension) Any (personal) firearm with such a mechanism.
Synonym of revolving line of credit
Agent noun of revolve; something that revolves.
To bring (something) in from a foreign country, especially for sale or trade.
To load a file into a software application from another version or system.
definitions.example: How can I import files from older versions of this application?

definitions.definition: To be important; to be significant; to be of consequence.
To be of importance to (someone or something).
To be incumbent on (someone to do something).
To be important or crucial to (that something happen).
To mean, signify.
To express, to imply.
